name: Kaunas County (Lithuania)

colors.symbolism: the standard outer field of Lithuanian county flags
the field of the Kaunas coat of arms
the silver bull of the Kaunas coat of arms
the gold cross above the bull

description: A blue field with a red inner panel bearing the coat of arms of Kaunas County: a silver wild bull with a golden cross between its horns.
history: Like the flags of all ten Lithuanian counties, Kaunas County's flag follows the uniform template introduced after the country's redivision into apskritys (counties) in 1994: a blue outer border surrounding the county's heraldic field, here red, charged with the historic Kaunas arms.

features.symbolism: blue outer border in the Lithuanian county pattern, with a red inner field bearing the county arms

features.role: coat of arms of Kaunas County
features.symbolism: a silver wild bull (aurochs) with a golden cross between its horns on a red field — the historic arms of the city and county of Kaunas
